behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between request builder and API client (MOCK-MISS yes: concrete cross-module invariant is taxonomy list in outbound payload). No real OS/network is required; a stub transport can assert payload exactly (REAL-INFRA no). No dependence on model judgment (LLM-DEP no). Rejected higher level: behavior text is about request contents ('request sent ... includes ... labels'), not live model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake API client that records outbound request. Invoke classify() with a known taxonomy and assert the captured payload contains the full label set (no missing/extra labels), proving construction-to-dispatch handoff correctness.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Bug can occur at response-handoff path (API adapter -> validator -> return value), not only pure parsing (MOCK-MISS yes). A stubbed API response with invalid label can reproduce real failure; real network/OS not required (REAL-INFRA no). No model-quality dependence because response is fixed fixture (LLM-DEP no). Rejected Agentic with behavior text: this checks fallback mapping for 'API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy', not whether model chooses correct labels.
behaviors.test_description: Use module with fake API returning an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., 'medical'). Assert classify() returns 'unknown' and never exposes raw invalid label.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly one-function determinism ('normalization step') with fixed input/output and no module handoff required (MOCK-MISS no). No real infra (REAL-INFRA no), no model dependence (LLM-DEP no), no multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixtures (case/whitespace variants included) and assert identical normalized label every time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live model calls and consistency under potential model variability (LLM-DEP yes). A stub would always pass and hide true inconsistency risk (MOCK-HIDE yes). Not Workflow: it is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-stage agent pipeline (MULTI-STEP no; disqualifying text is singular operation 'live API calls ... returned label').
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real provider calls for identical document+taxonomy, record raw provider outputs and module final labels, and assert final-label changes only occur when raw provider response changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure is commonly at module boundary between budget calculator/policy and dispatcher (MOCK-MISS yes: state handoff could send untrimmed content). Can be caught with in-process fake client observing what is dispatched; no real OS/network needed (REAL-INFRA no). No need for real model outputs (LLM-DEP no). Rejected System/Agentic: behavior text is 'before dispatch' policy enforcement, not provider behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure tight token budget and policy (truncate or reject), invoke classify() with oversized document, and assert either (a) dispatched request is truncated within budget or (b) call is rejected before any dispatch, using a fake client to verify no over-budget send.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Needs real external API metering to catch mismatches between local expectation and provider-reported usage; mocks can hide true overage (REAL-INFRA yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Agentic despite live AI call: disqualifying text is about 'provider-reported token usage ... within budget', which tests usage accounting/infrastructure, not model decision quality/content.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real API call with representative document and configured budget, read provider usage metadata from actual response, and assert reported total tokens <= configured per-call budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration across local modules (client adapter, backoff policy, caller contract) with a concrete boundary invariant: on rate-limit signal, perform delay+retry then succeed/finalize. In-process fake API can deterministically emit rate-limit then success; no real OS/network required (REAL-INFRA no). No model-output dependence (LLM-DEP no). Rejected System: behavior text focuses on control flow ('waits and retries') not real network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API that returns rate-limit error on first call and success on second; use controllable clock/backoff. Assert module performs at least one wait, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing immediate error.
